codeReview.summary: Reviewed the Settings browser verification follow-up against the localized Settings implementation commit and worker evidence. The feature stayed in scope, reused the existing Settings localization changes without modifying code, and the recorded browser checks match the implemented localized header, tabs, and covered top-level sections with persisted locale and html lang behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo still has an already-known machine-specific test-path failure that workers repeatedly rediscover during validation, but the shared library docs do not record it as reusable environment knowledge.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: settings-browser-verification-pass handoff and prior workspace-settings-core-copy handoffs both document `test/conversation-output-cleaning.test.ts` failing because it expects `/Users/mybiblepath/Development/cabinet/...` instead of this workspace path; `.factory/library/` has no note about this baseline debt.

summary: Pass. The feature correctly performed a no-code browser verification pass for the existing Settings localization, captured evidence for zh/en header, tabs, and covered sections, and did not expand scope. No correctness or regression issues were identified in the reviewed implementation or verification flow.
